question: How do I create a new AI agent in Odoo 19?
expected_answer: Go to AI > Agents > Agents and click Create. Set the agent name, system prompt, welcome message, default model, and provider. Choose allowed tools and visibility. Save and deploy to a Discuss channel, activity type, or task if needed.

question: What is the difference between an AI provider and an AI model in Odoo?
expected_answer: An AI provider (ai.provider) holds the connection settings and credentials for an LLM service such as OpenAI or Ollama. An AI model (ai.model) represents a specific model name (e.g., gpt-4o) with metadata like context window, pricing, and modality, and is linked to one provider.

question: How do I connect Odoo to Azure OpenAI?
expected_answer: Create an ai.provider with provider_type='azure_openai'. Enter the Azure base URL, deployment name, API version, and API key. Then create an ai.model linked to that provider with the exact deployment model name and context window. Assign the model to an agent.

question: How can I restrict which tools an AI agent is allowed to use?
expected_answer: On the ai.agent form, in the Tools tab, select only the ai.tool records the agent should use. The agent will receive JSON schemas only for those tools during the function-calling loop. Ensure the tool records exist and have valid schemas.

question: How do I deploy an AI agent to a Discuss channel?
expected_answer: Open the ai.agent record, use the Deploy smart button or channel_ids field, and select the Discuss channels where the agent should participate. Set visibility so the agent responds to mentions or all messages depending on channel needs.

question: How does human handoff work for AI agents in Odoo?
expected_answer: Human handoff is configured via the handoff_threshold field on ai.agent or through escalation rules. When the threshold is reached (e.g., misunderstood messages, sensitive topic detected, user explicitly asks for human), the agent stops responding and notifies assigned operators via Discuss or activities.

question: What security groups control access to AI providers and credentials?
expected_answer: ai.group_ai_user can use agents; ai.group_ai_manager can configure agents, prompts, and tools; ai.group_ai_admin can manage providers, encrypted credentials, usage reports, and security settings. Record rules further restrict credential read access.

question: How do I use AI prompts for content generation?
expected_answer: Create an ai.prompt record with the desired template. The content field supports o_editor_prompt HTML divs. Define variables via variable_ids. At runtime, render the prompt with record context and pass it to an agent or generation method. Prompts can be versioned and assigned to agents.

question: How is conversation history managed for AI agents?
expected_answer: Conversation history is stored in ai_message records linked to an ai.agent.session. The session is linked to a Discuss channel or a record. The agent includes up to max_history recent messages in the LLM context. Old conversations can be summarized when approaching the model context window.

question: How do I monitor AI usage and costs in Odoo?
expected_answer: Usage is tracked in ai.usage.log or ai_message records with token counts and linked provider/model. Configure usage limits per user/provider/company. A cron job can generate periodic usage reports and send alerts when limits are approached.
